## [00:00 - 03:09]

Hey folks, it's Jeff Walker and I have Sebastian Knight here for a key study. I've gotten to know Sebastian over the last few years and he's doing some really cool stuff and I know you're going to get a lot out of this. So first of all, Sebastian's from France and in fact he's got a great story. So excuse me for that. Yeah, I think he's got a great story about the Eiffel Tower. I think hopefully we'll get to. But he's done launches in more than one market and he's been doing this for a while. So you originally bought Prack Launch Formula 2. So that was a few years ago and take us forward from there. What happened? So I had at the time a very small business where I did personal coaching for people who were shy because that was my problem a while before. So basically that's what you would call dating advice. I would help people get over their frustrations and anxiety. Specifically men? Yeah, men. I would help men that would not be able to talk to a woman. I would get them to be able to take numbers, updates and get the load of their life. And I did workshops at the time. But the thing is my goal in life was to make about 3 grand in euros that would be less than $4,000 a month. And that would be enough to pay for all of my expenses and everything. And at the time that I bought PLF, I was just a bit over that because I had discovered marketing. And I was making maybe six grand a month. And then I bought PLF and I said, okay, I need to bring this to the next level and see what happens. The fun thing is I bought PLF 2, but I did not use PLF 2 at the time because there was a booklet that you gave as a bonus, the previous version, the very first version of PLF. And when I got the book, I got so busy reading it. And I think that's things that you now have in the PDF transcripts. But I got so busy reading the book that I didn't even have the time to log in and check all of the videos. So I had like 25% or 20% of the whole thing. But I started doing the launch. And since I was not very, let's say, so good of myself, I was like trying to take baby steps into the process. I did the first launch. It took me four months pre-launch. So basically every time I would talk about one of my workshops, I would say, hey, by the way, I'm still working on this thing that's coming up. And then I would give people a PDF or a video about that. But I was afraid to release it to the world. You're not the first person. I mean, I've been there. So I was a bit holding back on launching it. And I had heard at the time of launch in the US where they had 17,000 people on their list when they did the launch.

## [09:11 - 12:18]

## [15:20 - 18:28]

And I'd like to work with you to try to make a product, launch it and get some sales out of it. So he's seeing you basically your success, what you have been doing. The book is obvious. So we had an article... The first stroke of genius. The idea was, okay, a list of zero is not good. We can do better. I said, there's no way we can do worse. That's the good news. So let's see what we can do with it. So he posted an article saying, hey, I just did this podcast on how the seven ways to do XYZ. So just give me your email below and I will send it by email. So what we decided to do is try to do a program not on how to build a business, not on how to make more money, but just how to overcome procrastination. Very specialized. How to overcome procrastination when you want to start your own business. This really tiny segment of the market. And the list grew over one month, pretty much, to 300, a bit over 300 people.

## [34:27 - 37:28]

people in France are not as used to buying online so the truth is when I launched my first online thing I hadn't myself bought anything online the first time I sold my first workshop online I was still having a friend buy things for me and then cutting a check to them because I was offering to put my own credit card online so that was like the epitome of how the French people some of them are still scared of being online so that was amazing to see that because we had all of this pre-launch process and I have had customers telling me that they are not confident with the whole buying something on the internet process but because of the pre-launch content in all these markets we were able to project to people hey I'm a real person you've seen me even if it's just a short video because the rest of the launch is email but just to say hey I'm a real person you can email back to me I will answer on the blog or I will survey you on any survey service just that gives people enough confidence to say okay my money is not going out to the cyberspace and disappearing so that was a big thing so across all these markets and all these launches you've done what's one of the biggest takeaways in terms of launching them? I've had some mistakes in my own launches and I've seen launches where I have been called at the last hour to like try to fix it! I had a friend launch a product and for the first launch day he made one sale and he said okay what's the problem and when we fixed it he made 20 sales in the same amount of time and the thing is there are five things and I have them on like a checklist to make sure that I never make there are just five mistakes that can be done in a launch in my opinion because people don't care if you messed up in an email or messed up a link or if the video is slow to download that's not a problem to me like the five things I've identified so far are if a launch doesn't work out look at the offer is the offer clear? is it clear you're going to get this for this price? you're going to get it in this fashion online or physical product and you're going to pay in this fashion if that's clear that's the first thing second thing is the offer what they wanted because you cannot it's really hard to fail when you've asked people what they want and you've given them what they want

## [37:28 - 40:30]

like no brainer but sometimes I've seen people try to sell what they want to sell and I've seen in the front space some guy with the guy has a blog on finance and he wants to sell a product on the Zen mindset doesn't work that good because it's like people there's a disconnect between what people wanted and what they sell the third thing is the offer really irresistible like is it a lot of value for a small price and what I've been always pushing for is like I want to give four times the value for whatever I ask being the price and I think I learned that from seeing the way you structured when I first bought Pia, I've seen the way you structured the Pia offer and I was like can this be worth the amount I'm paying for my business? Yes, can this be worth two or three or four times later to my business? and it was worth it so that's why I bought it so I said maybe I should do the same thing when I'm offering a product and the other thing is the other major mistake is is there a congruency throughout the launch? and there's a funny story, I don't know if I can talk about it, a story of a failed launch because when I launched my marketing strategy product in the French market I had a few months later, two months later, one of the big guys in that market with a pretty large list launched a product by copying my own sequence it was like okay pretty much the same thing like VJV emails because I was on the JV list so the JV emails you could put them side by side and it's exactly the same thing the bold and italics and dashes and everything is the same in the JV emails and the promotions, the video scripts are pretty much the same the sales video was the same script like work for work, same script because you had a printer so you can actually do work for work, same script changing like the product name, his name, the price and removing the guarantee on top of that but other than that the whole thing was just a Xerox copy of what I had done and the guy had a huge list and on launch day he made zero sales and I think he made one sale in the night before I realized because I was doing a seminar at the time and when I come back home and I look at the sales video and I'm like, I've heard this before he's like, I've heard this before I've said it before so I'm like okay, there's a problem so I had this, the thing shut down

## [40:30 - 43:32]

the reason, in my opinion why it didn't work is because there was no sincerity in the launch it's like if you see a chocolate cake and you don't know the recipe and you're not a cook you can maybe do something that looks like a chocolate cake out of platter and paint and stuff like that, it can look the same but since you don't have the formula, the recipe to do it you're not going to get the same taste to it and so that's, for me that's a big deal I'm not trying to copy paste other launches that I've seen it's like trying to actually get a relationship with the people that are going to experience the launch like okay, what do they want and then being genuine in that relationship because that's a big part of it people when they are going full launch as the prospect they're like okay, can I trust this person is this really made for, in order to help me or is this made in order to make a buck and I think people have a BS detector that goes off big time when they sense that you're not doing this to help them, you're doing this to get the money so yeah, that would be a big one and I think that's fine and the word you used was the congruity I think that's the word you used and ## [43:32 - 44:34]

like it was the same for the book or DVD whatever the product is I always try to think how can I add more value upfront so that people are happy to give the money because they say ok I'm going to get even more value out of it so even when I have a very short lead time and that happens sometimes you forget that you had a contract that needs to go out on Saturday and it's Friday morning and you're like ok I need to sell this tomorrow I always try to send some value upfront and to get people involved in the process before trying to pitch them something so that made a tremendous difference in my income and in my life, my personal life and the ones of my customers and my clients so that's the thing that's most important for me so thanks a lot for that You're welcome, you're welcome this awesome live ear that love hearing that kind of stuff so thank you very much
